prompt:
Analytics-platform ticket. Our usage-reporting service talks to BigQuery through a pile of copy-pasted fetch calls that string-interpolate values straight into SQL, silently truncate any result past the first response page, and turn 64-bit user IDs into corrupted doubles because someone fed them through Number(). I want this rebuilt properly as a small TypeScript module family under bq/ — no npm packages, Node 26 built-ins only (native fetch, node:test runs the .ts files directly). test_bqrunner.ts is the acceptance contract.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official BigQuery REST reference — at minimum the jobs.query method page (QueryRequest/QueryResponse shapes, what jobComplete=false implies), the jobs.getQueryResults method page (query parameters, when schema/rows are present, pageToken semantics), and the parameterized-queries guide (named parameter JSON shapes, the NULL rule, the GoogleSQL requirement). Only first-party Google pages count as sources. Our pinned wire contract lives in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected fixtures. Compare the live official docs against them — if anything disagrees, say so explicitly in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What the tests import: runQuery, BigQueryJobError, and BigQueryHttpError from ./bq/client.ts, and encodeParam from ./bq/params.ts. Split the rest sensibly (row/schema decoding deserves its own module).

- runQuery(cfg, sql, params?, opts?) — cfg carries baseUrl, projectId, token, location, pollTimeoutMs, maxPolls. Submit via the documented jobs.query POST: bearer auth, JSON body with useLegacySql false (read the parameterized-queries guide for why that's not optional), parameterMode and queryParameters ONLY when parameters were actually given, plus maxResults/timeoutMs/location. timeoutMs is the injected wait — the client itself never sleeps or looks at a clock.
- Named parameters from a plain object: strings, bigints, numbers, booleans, and one-level arrays of those. Follow the documented scalar and ARRAY encodings exactly, and reject locally (before any request) the two cases the docs rule out — you'll find them on the parameterized-queries page.
- If the response says the job isn't done, poll the documented results endpoint with the returned jobReference (its location matters on every call), bounded by maxPolls; give up with an error mentioning the job is still incomplete. Remember what the reference says about schema/rows on an incomplete response — there is nothing to decode yet.
- Page rows with the opaque continuation token exactly as documented until the final page; never rebuild it. Count pages, and parse totalRows into the only JS type that can hold it faithfully.
- Decode rows against the schema, preserving what the wire actually carries: the f/v cell structure, NULL cells, REPEATED cells (including empty ones), 64-bit integers that don't fit in a double (they must come back exact), floats, and the string-carried booleans.
- Job-level errors in a response must fail the run BEFORE any rows are surfaced or any further page is fetched — BigQueryJobError with the decoded reason/location/message list and the jobId. Non-2xx responses raise BigQueryHttpError with the API's error message and status; the bearer token must never appear in any error text.

Acceptance: node --test test_bqrunner.ts — the tests run a local node:http mock, no real project or credentials anywhere. test_bqrunner.ts and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.interface: BigQuery REST API v2, jobs.query and jobs.getQueryResults

official_sources.title: Method: jobs.query | BigQuery | Google Cloud
official_sources.used_for: POST /projects/{projectId}/queries; QueryRequest fields query, useLegacySql, parameterMode (NAMED/POSITIONAL), queryParameters ({parameterType, parameterValue}), maxResults, timeoutMs, location, requestId, dryRun; QueryResponse fields kind, schema, jobReference, jobComplete, totalRows (STRING-encoded), pageToken, rows (f/v encoding), errors, cacheHit; jobComplete=false means the query is still running and results come later via jobs.getQueryResults with the jobReference

official_sources.title: Method: jobs.getQueryResults | BigQuery | Google Cloud
official_sources.used_for: GET /projects/{projectId}/queries/{jobId} with location, maxResults, pageToken, startIndex, timeoutMs query parameters; GetQueryResultsResponse with jobComplete (false → schema/rows absent), schema/rows only when complete, pageToken present until the final page, totalRows as unsigned int64 string, errors as job-level ErrorProto list, cacheHit, numDmlAffectedRows

official_sources.title: Use parameterized queries | BigQuery | Google Cloud
official_sources.used_for: named parameters referenced as @name with parameterMode NAMED; scalar shape {name, parameterType: {type: "STRING"}, parameterValue: {value: ...}}; array shape {parameterType: {type: "ARRAY", arrayType: {type: ...}}, parameterValue: {arrayValues: [{value: ...}]}}; 'Query parameters are only supported in GoogleSQL syntax' (useLegacySql: false required); 'A query parameter value can't be NULL'

research.language_rationale: The consumer is a TypeScript reporting service, and the catalog item targets the documented REST v2 wire contract itself — the f/v row encoding, string-typed totalRows/INT64 cells, jobComplete gating, and opaque pageToken continuation that the official @google-cloud/bigquery npm package deliberately abstracts away. Node 26's built-in fetch, node:http, and node:test cover the documented surface with zero dependencies, and bigint is the only faithful decoding for BigQuery's 64-bit integers in JavaScript.
